name: technology
label: Software Engineering — Tech Knowledge Base
description: Programming languages, frameworks, concepts and best practices.
observations: Python is a high-level interpreted programming language
Python is widely used for machine learning and data science
Rust is a systems programming language focused on memory safety
Rust achieves memory safety without a garbage collector
TypeScript is a statically typed superset of JavaScript
React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces
React was created by Facebook and open-sourced in 2013
FastAPI is a modern Python web framework for building APIs
FastAPI is based on Pydantic and uses type hints for validation
PostgreSQL is an open-source relational database
PostgreSQL supports JSON natively making it useful for flexible schemas
Redis is an in-memory data store used for caching
Docker packages applications into portable containers
Kubernetes orchestrates containers at scale
GraphQL is a query language for APIs developed by Facebook
REST APIs communicate over HTTP using standard methods
Machine learning models learn patterns from training data
Neural networks are inspired by the structure of the human brain
Large language models like GPT are trained on vast text datasets
Transformers use attention mechanisms to process sequences
Vector databases store embeddings for similarity search
RAG stands for Retrieval Augmented Generation
RAG retrieves relevant documents before generating a response
Embeddings represent text as dense numerical vectors
Git is a distributed version control system created by Linus Torvalds
CI/CD automates the process of testing and deploying code
Microservices split applications into small independent services
An API gateway handles routing and authentication for microservices
SOLID principles guide object-oriented software design
Test-driven development writes tests before writing code
test_queries: What is the difference between REST and GraphQL?
